content.text:
Build succeeds with no errors. All changes are in place:

1. **EditorProvider** - Added `maximizedPanel: null` to initial state and `TOGGLE_MAXIMIZE_PANEL` reducer case
2. **App.module.css** - Added `.workspaceMaximized`, `.previewHidden`, `.primaryCanvasHidden` classes
3. **App.jsx** - Reads `maximizedPanel`, conditionally renders panels, passes `isMaximized`/`onToggleMaximize` to ThreePreviewPanel
4. **ToolbarIcons.jsx** - Added `ExpandIcon` and `CollapseIcon` components
5. **SvgCanvas.jsx** - Added expand/collapse button above the status bar
6. **SvgCanvas.module.css** - Added `.expandBtn` styles
7. **ThreePreviewPanel.jsx** - Added expand/collapse button in the header actions

The toggle works by conditionally unmounting the non-active panel (rather than just hiding with CSS), which ensures the 3D preview's ResizeObserver fires correctly when restoring. Clicking the expand button on either panel maximizes it to full width; clicking again restores the split view.

message.content: ._button_15ip8_56 the icon here is not showing

content.text: Let me read the existing plan and investigate the icon issue.
The issue is that the SVG icon has no intrinsic size — it relies on a CSS class for sizing. Let me check how other icons are sized.

content.content:
C:\Users\johnm\modular apartment planner\src\ui\Toolbar.module.css:71:.icon {

[Showing results with pagination = limit: 20, offset: 0]

content.text:
Found the issue. The `I` component in `ToolbarIcons.jsx` uses `className={className || 'icon'}` — but `'icon'` is a plain string, and the `.icon` class that sizes icons is defined in `Toolbar.module.css` (line 71-75), which gets scoped by CSS Modules. So when the icon is rendered inside ThreePreviewPanel's `.button`, the SVG gets `className="icon"` which matches nothing — it has no width/height.

The SvgCanvas expand button works because we added `.expandBtn svg { width: 14px; height: 14px; }`. The ThreePreviewPanel button needs the same treatment.

input.new_string:
# Fix: Icon not visible in ThreePreviewPanel expand button

## Context
The fullscreen toggle feature was just implemented. The expand/collapse button in the ThreePreviewPanel header (`.button` class, selector `._button_15ip8_56`) renders an `ExpandIcon`/`CollapseIcon` SVG, but the icon is invisible because it has no sizing.

**Root cause:** `ToolbarIcons.jsx` `I` component defaults to `className="icon"` (a plain string), but the `.icon` class that sizes icons (18x18px) lives in `Toolbar.module.css` — CSS Modules scopes it, so the plain `"icon"` class matches nothing. The SVG renders at 0x0.

The SvgCanvas expand button works fine because `SvgCanvas.module.css` already has `.expandBtn svg { width: 14px; height: 14px; }`.

## Fix

**File: `src/three/viewer/ThreePreviewPanel.module.css`**

Add an SVG sizing rule inside `.button`:

```css
.button svg {
  width: 16px;
  height: 16px;
  display: block;
}
```

This targets the SVG child of any `.button` in the panel. The "Reset View" button has no SVG child so it's unaffected.

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` — open app
2. Verify the expand icon is now visible in the 3D Preview header next to "Reset View"
3. Verify "Reset View" button text still renders correctly (no visual regression)
4. Click the expand button — panel maximizes, icon switches to collapse
5. Click collapse — split view restores

# Fix: Icon not visible in ThreePreviewPanel expand button

## Context
The fullscreen toggle feature was just implemented. The expand/collapse button in the ThreePreviewPanel header (`.button` class, selector `._button_15ip8_56`) renders an `ExpandIcon`/`CollapseIcon` SVG, but the icon is invisible because it has no sizing.

**Root cause:** `ToolbarIcons.jsx` `I` component defaults to `className="icon"` (a plain string), but the `.icon` class that sizes icons (18x18px) lives in `Toolbar.module.css` — CSS Modules scopes it, so the plain `"icon"` class matches nothing. The SVG renders at 0x0.

The SvgCanvas expand button works fine because `SvgCanvas.module.css` already has `.expandBtn svg { width: 14px; height: 14px; }`.

## Fix

**File: `src/three/viewer/ThreePreviewPanel.module.css`**

Add an SVG sizing rule inside `.button`:

```css
.button svg {
  width: 16px;
  height: 16px;
  display: block;
}
```

This targets the SVG child of any `.button` in the panel. The "Reset View" button has no SVG child so it's unaffected.

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` — open app
2. Verify the expand icon is now visible in the 3D Preview header next to "Reset View"
3. Verify "Reset View" button text still renders correctly (no visual regression)
4. Click the expand button — panel maximizes, icon switches to collapse
5. Click collapse — split view restores

lines: +# Fix: Icon not visible in ThreePreviewPanel expand button

-The modular apartment planner has a split model workspace: SVG plan canvas (left) and 3D preview panel (right) in a CSS grid. Users want to be able to maximize either panel to use the full canvas area, then toggle back. Currently there is no expand/maximize functionality.

+The fullscreen toggle feature was just implemented. The expand/collapse button in the ThreePreviewPanel header (`.button` class, selector `._button_15ip8_56`) renders an `ExpandIcon`/`CollapseIcon` SVG, but the icon is invisible because it has no sizing.

-Add a `maximizedPanel` state to EditorProvider (`null | 'canvas' | 'preview'`). When set, the `.modelWorkspace` grid hides the non-maximized panel and the active one takes the full width. Each panel gets a small expand/collapse toggle button.

+**Root cause:** `ToolbarIcons.jsx` `I` component defaults to `className="icon"` (a plain string), but the `.icon` class that sizes icons (18x18px) lives in `Toolbar.module.css` — CSS Modules scopes it, so the plain `"icon"` class matches nothing. The SVG renders at 0x0.
+The SvgCanvas expand button works fine because `SvgCanvas.module.css` already has `.expandBtn svg { width: 14px; height: 14px; }`.
-- Add `maximizedPanel: null` to initial state in `createInitialEditorState` (line ~217)
-Add modifier classes that override `.modelWorkspace` grid when a panel is maximized:

-Note: CSS Modules scopes class names, so we can't use nested `.preview` selectors directly. Instead, add standalone `.previewHidden` and `.primaryCanvasHidden` classes with `display: none`, and apply them conditionally in JSX.
+This targets the SVG child of any `.button` in the panel. The "Reset View" button has no SVG child so it's unaffected.
-Add a small expand/collapse button in the status bar area (bottom-right corner of the canvas container). The button sits inside `.canvasContainer` as an absolutely positioned element.
-- Add expand/collapse button in the `.actions` div (next to "Reset View"):
-| `src/app/App.jsx` | Read `maximizedPanel`, conditionally hide panels, pass props to ThreePreviewPanel |
-| `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` | Add expand button in canvas container |
-| `src/three/viewer/ThreePreviewPanel.jsx` | Add expand button in header actions |
-1. `npm run dev` - open app in browser
-2. In plan view, verify split layout shows SVG canvas + 3D preview side by side
-3. Click expand button on SVG canvas -> 3D preview disappears, canvas takes full width
-4. Click collapse button -> split view restores
-5. Click expand button on 3D preview header -> SVG canvas disappears, preview takes full width
-6. Click collapse on preview -> split view restores
-7. Switch to elevation/section view -> maximize state persists correctly
-8. Switch to sheet workspace -> maximize doesn't apply (sheets have no split)
-9. Verify 3D preview ResizeObserver fires correctly when maximizing/restoring (viewport resizes properly)
-10. Verify keyboard shortcuts still work in both states
+2. Verify the expand icon is now visible in the 3D Preview header next to "Reset View"
+3. Verify "Reset View" button text still renders correctly (no visual regression)
+4. Click the expand button — panel maximizes, icon switches to collapse

content.text: Simple fix — just one CSS rule needed. The plan file is updated.

input.plan:
# Fix: Icon not visible in ThreePreviewPanel expand button

## Context
The fullscreen toggle feature was just implemented. The expand/collapse button in the ThreePreviewPanel header (`.button` class, selector `._button_15ip8_56`) renders an `ExpandIcon`/`CollapseIcon` SVG, but the icon is invisible because it has no sizing.

**Root cause:** `ToolbarIcons.jsx` `I` component defaults to `className="icon"` (a plain string), but the `.icon` class that sizes icons (18x18px) lives in `Toolbar.module.css` — CSS Modules scopes it, so the plain `"icon"` class matches nothing. The SVG renders at 0x0.

The SvgCanvas expand button works fine because `SvgCanvas.module.css` already has `.expandBtn svg { width: 14px; height: 14px; }`.

## Fix

**File: `src/three/viewer/ThreePreviewPanel.module.css`**

Add an SVG sizing rule inside `.button`:

```css
.button svg {
  width: 16px;
  height: 16px;
  display: block;
}
```

This targets the SVG child of any `.button` in the panel. The "Reset View" button has no SVG child so it's unaffected.

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` — open app
2. Verify the expand icon is now visible in the 3D Preview header next to "Reset View"
3. Verify "Reset View" button text still renders correctly (no visual regression)
4. Click the expand button — panel maximizes, icon switches to collapse
5. Click collapse — split view restores
